description_long: This object is a freestanding water cooler with a rectangular, light gray base and a large, cylindrical blue water bottle mounted on top. The front face features a recessed dispensing area with two metallic spouts and a small drip tray below, designed for filling cups or bottles. The sides and back of the base are plain and unadorned, emphasizing the utilitarian design. The water bottle is semi-transparent, suggesting it is made of plastic and filled with water. The overall construction appears sturdy, with the base likely made of plastic or metal for durability in public or office environments. No visible branding or cup holder attachments are present on the exterior.
description: A freestanding water cooler with a blue bottle on top and a gray rectangular base, featuring two spouts and a drip tray on the front. The design is simple and functional, suitable for public or office use.
description_view_0: Front view showing the blue water bottle, dispensing area with two spouts, and a drip tray.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the plain, rectangular gray base and the cylindrical blue bottle, with no visible controls or features.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the back of the water cooler, which is featureless and smooth.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, with no visible controls or features.
attribute_control_panel_design: Simple recessed area with two metallic lever spouts and a drip tray; no visible buttons or indicators.
